spoken_input:
It found a flawed launch decision process. It found communication failures. It found that crucial information about O-ring history, temperature concern, and continuing engineering opposition did not reach key decision-makers with the force and clarity it needed.

The people closest to the hardware had the sharpest version of the risk. The people with the authority to stop launch received a softened version of it.

The Commission described the immediate failure: the part, the launch decision, and the communication system that softened the warning.

Diane Vaughan later named the larger pattern: the normalization of deviance. A deviation from expected performance becomes normal because the system keeps surviving it.

The phrase is useful because it does not require a conspiracy. It describes a slow adjustment in what feels acceptable. The line moves because each previous crossing did not end in catastrophe.

Challenger is taught in engineering programs, business schools, and organizational behavior courses because the pattern is not limited to NASA.

Repeated near-misses create false confidence. Every time a system survives a close call, perceived risk can drop. The near-miss gets counted as validation instead of warning.

Missing data gets mistaken for safety. No evidence of failure is not the same as evidence of no failure, especially when the dangerous condition has not really been tested.

Schedule pressure changes proof standards quietly. No one has to announce that the bar has moved. The bar for stopping can rise inch by inch until only certainty of disaster justifies delay.

Institutions reward continuity over interruption. The person who keeps the line moving is easier to celebrate than the person who stops it for a risk that might never become visible.

Anomalies become background noise. The first time something unexpected happens, it gets attention. The fifth time, a tracking number. The tenth time, it starts to sound like how the system works.

These behaviors appear wherever the cost of stopping is immediate and the cost of continuing is statistical. Healthcare systems. Financial systems. Infrastructure. Software. Any organization that has to decide whether a warning is strong enough to interrupt momentum.

Challenger did not invent this pattern. It made the pattern visible.

previous_text:
gan appointed the Rogers Commission, chaired by William Rogers. Richard Feynman was one of its members, and his independent work became part of the public memory of the investigation.

The physical cause was direct: failure of the seal in the right booster's aft field joint, hot gas escaping, damage to the External Tank area, and vehicle breakup.

The Commission did not stop at the physical cause.
next_text:
Disasters rarely begin at the moment of impact. They begin when a system gets used to explaining away what it should have stopped to understand.

Challenger was a long sequence of accommodations, each one easier to defend than the whole chain would have been.
